textDisplay:
I am from India, and my aunts tell me this story from the mid 80's when one of my Uncles, brought over a VHS tape, without a cover. He was trying to learn English by watching Hollywood Films. One afternoon he came back with a VHS tape without any cover and title 'College Girls'. He called my two aunts (19 and 22 at that time) and proudly proclaimed, "Sisters, I have this movie that is about college girls, just like you.". The whole family gathered to watch it. The tape was played and promptly destroyed within 10 minutes of its viewing. 

I dont know why this trailer reminded me of that story.

textDisplay:
If this was about a 60 year old man cheating on his hot, faithful wife and mother of his children with a 20 year old female intern, who would be producing that film today and giving it great reviews?  This is just another example of Hollywood figuring out what Harlequin romance novels have known forever- that women struggle with lust, too.  And the older we get, we struggle to feel like we are seen and wanted.  The answer to that struggle, though, is not to watch trash like this and pretend we're Nicole Kidman and that we're wanted by men old enough to be our grandsons.  The answer is to see that are valuable just as we are- and wanted by the God who created us, and that is where we get our worth, not from some dude wanting to stick his hand up our skirt and feed us dog treats.
Let's to better, ladies.
textOriginal:
If this was about a 60 year old man cheating on his hot, faithful wife and mother of his children with a 20 year old female intern, who would be producing that film today and giving it great reviews?  This is just another example of Hollywood figuring out what Harlequin romance novels have known forever- that women struggle with lust, too.  And the older we get, we struggle to feel like we are seen and wanted.  The answer to that struggle, though, is not to watch trash like this and pretend we're Nicole Kidman and that we're wanted by men old enough to be our grandsons.  The answer is to see that are valuable just as we are- and wanted by the God who created us, and that is where we get our worth, not from some dude wanting to stick his hand up our skirt and feed us dog treats.
Let's to better, ladies.

textDisplay:
Loving the Pavlovian bell throughout the trailer.

>The discovery of classical conditioning was accidental. While researching the digestion of dogs, Pavlov observed that the dogs' physical responses to food gradually changed. Initially, the dogs only salivated when the food was directly presented. However, he later noticed that the dogs began to salivate slightly before the food arrived in response to sounds consistently associated with feeding, such as the sound of the food cart. To test his theory, Pavlov conducted an experiment where he rang a bell shortly before presenting food to the dogs. Initially, the dogs did not salivate in response to the bell. However, as they learned to associate the sound of the bell with the arrival of food, they began to salivate at the sound alone.
